title: Tests of Fate
body: PLACEHOLDER SUMMARY. When an adventurer's action is uncertain, the GM calls for a test of fate. Draw a minor arcana card and add the relevant attribute (Swords, Pentacles, Cups, or Wands) to the card's value. A total of **14 or more** is a success; **13 or less** is a failure. Drawing the tested suit on the initial draw is a great success.

title: The Four Phases
body: PLACEHOLDER SUMMARY. Play cycles through four phases: the **Crawl Phase** (exploring the Underworld), the **Challenge Phase** (dangerous encounters), the **Camp Phase** (rest and recovery), and the **City Phase** (spending, healing, and preparing the next delve).

title: Attributes
body: PLACEHOLDER SUMMARY. Adventurers have four attributes, rated 1–4, matching the four suits. At creation you take the spread **4, 3, 2, 1**; your **4** goes to the suit of your chosen Path.

title: Card Values
body: PLACEHOLDER SUMMARY. Pips I–X are worth 1–10. Court cards: **Page 11, Knight 12, Queen 13, King 14**. The Fool is worth 0. Players draw from the minor arcana (plus the Fool); the GM draws from the major arcana (I–XXI).
